findings.evidence: `rg -n "principalDivisor|ofSupportFinite"` shows `@[expose] noncomputable def principalDivisor` at line 82 and the new body `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite ...` at line 83; the characteristic API is already `@[simp] lemma coeff_principalDivisor` at lines 85-88.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from `principalDivisor` and prove `coeff_principalDivisor` using `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite_coe` or an equivalent local proof instead of relying on public unfolding.
findings.issue: `OrderSystem.principalDivisor` is still `@[expose]`, making the new `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` body part of the public interface; this PR is specifically changing that body, and consumers should use `coeff_principalDivisor` rather than unfold the construction.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "principalHom|principalDivisor_add"` shows `@[expose] noncomputable def principalHom` at line 91, with public API lemmas `principalHom_apply` at lines 96-98 and `principalDivisor_add` at lines 104-106.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from `principalHom`; keep `principalHom_apply` and the map lemmas as the public interface.
findings.issue: `OrderSystem.principalHom` also exposes its constructor body even though `principalHom_apply`, `principalDivisor_zero`, and `principalDivisor_add` provide the needed hom API; exposing it lets users depend on the implementation packaging rather than the homomorphism contract.

summary: The refactor leaves implementation choices exposed as public API even though the file already provides characteristic lemmas, so downstream users can depend on the chosen `Finsupp` constructor rather than the divisor interface.
